data.body:
CALIBRATION-3 FLOOR-ATTRIBUTION EVIDENCE (design input for this story, per task 3dc0/relishable-ammonitic-hoverfly AC4; method pre-registered there: recompute impact_plan from each persisted verification.severity_attributes with the hard-override floor disabled; a block is floor-driven iff the floorless amplified impact falls below the finding's block_threshold while the floored value met it; thresholds from the persisted per-finding block_threshold, else current routing).

Corpus: plan-v2 segment, 1016 sidecars / 264 tickets / 2271 decision==block findings (every one carried severity_attributes).
- FLOOR-DRIVEN BLOCKS: 807 of 2271 = 35.5% of all blocking findings would NOT have blocked without the 0.85 hard override.
- ac_unverifiable-bearing blocks: 804; of these 395 are floor-driven. ac_unverifiable accounts for 48.9% of all floor-driven blocks (395/807) - the largest single override axis.
- GRADE DISTRIBUTION of the classified sample: the override fires at ANY non-none grade; in the seed-42 sample of 100 floor-driven ac_unverifiable findings, 52 fired at grade=low, 48 at grade=medium. The floor is currently grade-blind: 'low' buys the same 0.85 as 'high'.

ORACLE-KIND CLASSIFICATION (seed-42 random sample of 100 floor-driven ac_unverifiable findings; classified per the pre-registered protocol):
- underspecified_oracle: 56/100 - a check exists or is clearly constructible; the finding demands the exact command / file / expected value / definition (e.g. 'no concrete proving command named', 'default value never stated', ''render'' undefined').
- missing_oracle: 7/100 - the finding asserts no verification method exists at all (deliverable with no covering AC; 'verified only by human inspection'; non-durable external action).
- ambiguous: 37/100 - dominated by a THIRD failure mode the split design must decide on: the BROKEN oracle (a proving command/symbol/count that is factually wrong - nonexistent test paths, wrong CLI entry point, asserts 'exactly 4' where 5 exist, contradictory ACs). A broken oracle is arguably block-worthy (the stated verification CANNOT pass), unlike an underspecified one.

DESIGN IMPLICATIONS for the split: (1) the dominant floor-driven ac_unverifiable population (56%) is exactly the 'valid-but-terminal-detail' block class this story exists to demote - grading it below the blocking tier removes up to ~14% of ALL plan-v2 blocks (395 * 0.56 / 2271 ~ 9.7% conservatively from this axis alone, more if the other three override axes show the same pattern). (2) Consider a THREE-grade axis (missing / broken / underspecified) rather than two, or fold 'broken' into the floor-retaining grade: 37% of the sample is broken-oracle-shaped and demoting those would weaken a genuinely protective check. (3) The verify-pass rubric must define the grades with examples of each; the current binary fires floor-blind at grade=low in a majority of sampled cases.
